title: lupinus elegans var. exaltatus
intro: Lupinus elegans var. exaltatus, also known as the tall lupine, is a perennial plant species in the genus Lupinus. It is a legume that has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its high content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The tall lupine has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Lupinus elegans var. exaltatus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of menopause, among other uses.
